LOGIC ENGINE BY GLYPH SYSTEMS

Predeclared batch checks
Displayed-value rule: Every dollar figure is the full modeled savings before any commercial allocation. Fee-tier tables are mathematical illustrations, not contract terms or realized revenue.
Workload boundary: Program 38 uses the corrected Program 37 small-processor physics unchanged: 20 transactions per second baseline, 30-TPS shared physical ceiling, a 6,000-item queue limit, and sparse longer external-service disturbances with explicit clean gaps. The only experiment change is the precommitted ordinary-day profile mix. BURST_SATURATION is excluded by design.
Evidence boundary: This report measures modeled operational-loss avoidance in a controlled matched recovery harness. It does not claim market prediction, trading results, account access, realized customer savings, or electrical-energy superiority.

Executive result

Program 32 Full processed the same matched transfers, hidden service outcomes, physical capacity, queue limit, and restoration schedule as the Strong controller across 72 complete ten-minute rounds. Cumulative full modeled savings were $367.13, equal to $0.5099/min across all complete conditions.

This run contains only ordinary LOW, MODERATE, and HIGH conditions. The complete-round median was $0.3464/min, p10 was $0.0766/min, the worst complete round was $-0.0002/min, and 98.6% of complete rounds were positive.

Methodology and fairness

Each complete round lasts ten minutes: twenty seconds of clean Anchor calibration, a 520-second synthetic regional operating scenario containing explicit normal-operation gaps plus sparse disturbances, and a one-minute final clean drain. Every randomized twelve-round block contains exactly six LOW, four MODERATE, and two HIGH rounds. No BURST_SATURATION round is permitted.

The embedded Program 32 controller implementation, Stillness logic, memory/continuity rules, comparison controllers, workload physics, and frozen economic coefficients remain unchanged from corrected Program 37. Program 38 changes only the profile distribution. Every controller receives identical transfers, hidden outcomes, fault schedule, physical service ceiling, queue limit, retry limits, idempotency capability, visible state source, and restoration timing.

The future profile and seed sequence is committed before outcomes are observed. The evidence payload records a reproducible thirty-day schedule hash derived from the master seed.

The persistent track carries only clean Anchor and smoothed memory estimates across rounds. Transactions, queues, distress, tension, residue, Stillness state, and release state reset every round.
